corrections.text: The article states the model 'delivers approximately 289 tokens per second on output.' No cited source snapshot contains this specific figure. The four accessible snapshots (TechCrunch, BigGo Finance, FelloAI, LLM-Stats, and the Google official blog) all describe the model as '4x faster than comparable frontier models in output tokens per second' but none states a baseline or derives the 289 figure. The 4x-faster characterization is well-supported; the 289 t/s figure is not.

The article states Gemini 3.5 Flash 'is distributed across more than 230 countries and 70 languages, according to TechCrunch.' The TechCrunch source snapshot does not contain these specific figures. The Google official blog states the model is available 'to billions of people globally' without specifying country or language counts. The availability claim is plausible but the specific numbers and the TechCrunch attribution are unverified by the captured source snapshots.
